jobs.prompt: 16:9 educational editorial illustration for a university mathematical analysis lecture on the epsilon-delta definition of a function limit. Show a clean geometric scene that clearly communicates: when x enters a narrow vertical neighborhood around a point, the graph of the function stays inside a horizontal target band around the limit value. Use one smooth curve or ribbon-like graph crossing the scene, one clearly visible vertical window in the domain, and one horizontal value band; the implication should be visually understandable even without text. Warm paper background #faf9f5, ink-like structure #141413, dusty blue #6a9bcc, terracotta #d97757, olive #788c5d. Premium academic magazine style, lots of whitespace, minimal clutter, accurate in spirit, no readable text, no letters, no digits, no formulas, no fake axis labels, no UI.

16:9 abstract editorial illustration suggesting the two classic limits of analysis: oscillation nearly matching a straight guide (hinting sin x over x near 0) and gentle compounding growth toward a natural ceiling (hinting the number e). Same restrained palette (#faf9f5, #141413, #6a9bcc, #d97757, #788c5d), soft light, subtle paper grain. Abstract only: no digits, no letters, no equations, no fake charts.
